name: Chalk (Terminal String Styling)

performance.notes: Zero-cost ANSI escape code generation; negligible overhead

agent_readiness.notes: Most popular terminal string styling library. Chainable API: chalk.bold.red('Error'). Colors: red, green, blue, yellow, cyan, magenta, white, black. Modifiers: bold, dim, italic, underline, strikethrough, inverse. Background: bgRed, bgGreen, etc. 256 color and Truecolor support. Auto-detects terminal color support. v5 is ESM-only. v4 for CommonJS. Template literal tag support. Zero dependencies. Used by nearly every popular CLI tool.

gotchas: v5 is ESM-only — use v4 if CommonJS is required
Colors auto-disabled in non-TTY environments (can be forced)
No browser support (ANSI codes not rendered in browsers)
best_when: Any Node.js CLI tool needing colored terminal output
Status messages, error output, and progress indicators
Log output formatting for developer tools
avoid_when: Browser environments (use CSS or different library)
CommonJS projects on v5 (use v4 or kleur)
summary: Chalk is the most popular Node.js terminal string styling library. Chainable API: chalk.bold.red('Error'). Full color support (16, 256, Truecolor). Auto-detects terminal capabilities. Zero dependencies. Used by virtually every popular CLI tool. v5 is ESM-only (use v4 for CommonJS). Essential for any CLI tool wanting colored, styled terminal output.
